title: Asset Manager CSOP Lifts Options Cap for SK Hynix Fund
summary: CSOP Asset Management Ltd. is lifting a ceiling on the use of options in a $14.4 billion leveraged exchange-traded fund linked to South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix Inc., giving the fund manager more flexibility after a wild rally in the company’s shares.

title: Alhasan: Iran Has Held the Gulf States & Hormuz Hostage
summary:
Mediators say  US and Iran have made 'encouraging progress' in talks on a peace deal as technical-level discussions continue. Hasan Alhasan, Senior Fellow for Middle East Policy at the International Institute for Strategic Studies spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on US-Gulf relations going forward.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Declines, Stocks Diverge After US-Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
summary: Oil retreated while global stocks diverged as investors assessed early signs of progress in diplomatic talks between the US and Iran.

title: Chinese AI Stocks Rally on Demand Optimism and Policy Support
summary: Chinese artificial intelligence-related stocks jumped, with sentiment boosted by a more supportive policy tone from Beijing and ongoing global demand for the technology.

title: How Mike Henry Reshaped the World's Largest Mining Company
summary: As he prepares to step down after six years at the helm of BHP, Mike Henry joins Francine Lacqua to discuss the future of mining, the race for critical minerals and leadership lessons that shaped his tenure. Henry explains why copper has become one of the most strategic resources, how artificial intelligence and the energy transition are transforming demand and why BHP walked away from its pursuit of Anglo American. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Targets US Rare Earths Firms in Response to Pentagon List
summary: China has imposed export controls against two US rare earth producers that are part of Washington’s effort to reduce its dependence on Beijing by establishing an alternative supply chain for the critical minerals.

title: US and Iran Make ‘Progress’, Aim to Keep Hormuz Open
summary: The US and Iran made “encouraging progress” in talks on a peace deal and will continue technical-level discussions this week, according to mediators Qatar and Pakistan. Bloomberg's Stuart Livingstone-Wallace breaks down what we know so far. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Qatar Domestic Gas Plant Malfunction Blast Leaves 18 Missing
summary: Qatar said an incident during the startup of the Ras Laffan industrial complex resulted in a blast that has injured dozens, underscoring the risks to Middle East energy facilities as they ramp up production after the US-Iran ceasefire.

title: Global Banks Drive India Asset-Backed Securities Sales to Record
summary: India’s asset-backed securities market has surged to a record as global banks ramp up purchases to gain exposure to one of the world’s fastest-growing major economies.

title: Oil Declines After US-Iran Peace Talks Show Signs of Progress
summary: Oil dropped following signs of progress in peace talks between Washington and Tehran, which appeared to get off to a rocky start after US President Donald Trump issued a fresh threat against Iran.

title: Precious Shipping Sees Stronger Freight Rates For Longer
summary:
Precious Shipping MD Khalid Hashim says limited new vessel supply, combined with the resilience of older tonnage, is setting the stage for sustained strength in freight rates. He speaks with Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin."

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Masan Plans Tungsten Mine Expansion in Vietnam as It Courts Foreign Capital
summary: Masan High-Tech Materials announced an expansion of its Nui Phao mine and Nui Chiem area, potentially adding 115 million tons of tungsten-polymetallic resources as the company seeks to attract foreign investors and establish a stronger foothold in the global critical minerals supply chain.

title: Letta: Europe Needs To Be United In Front Of Trump
summary: Former Italian Prime Minister and IE School of Politics, Economics & Global Affairs Dean Enrico Letta, underscores the urgency of European unity in the face of Donald Trump’s presidency. Letta argues that only a cohesive and determined Europe can safeguard its interests and values in a shifting global landscape. He speaks with Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kuwait Adds to Signs Hormuz Is Reopening With Offer for Products
summary: Kuwait is asking customers to pick up refined petroleum from its ports deep inside the Persian Gulf, as the region’s oil giants try to ratchet up production and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz increases.

title: Olympian Eileen GU on Her Motivations for Success
summary: Olympic gold medalist Eileen Gu sits down exclusively with Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin to talk about her recent achievements, personal motivations and how she views her brand partnerships. The freestyle skier also shared why she prefers Peking Duck over Dim Sum, as well as why her favorite place is somewhere that is close to home. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chinese Oil Imports May Never Fully Recover From Iran War
summary: Chinese oil imports may never fully recover from the Iran war, with analysts saying the conflict accelerated a permanent shift away from fuels like gasoline and diesel.

title: Japan Insurers Sold Domestic Super-Long Bonds as Yields Soared
summary: Japan’s insurers sold domestic super-long government bonds in May, reversing course from the start of the fiscal year as yields climbed to multi-decade highs.

title: Emerging-Market Stocks Mark Fresh High as US-Iran Talks Progress, Oil Falls
summary: Emerging-market stocks rose to a fresh record as the US and Iran agreed upon a roadmap to a final peace deal, indicating progress in talks that have proceeded in fits and starts amid threats by President Donald Trump.

title: Oil Price Decline Could Remove Some Central Bank Hawkish Bias: Citigroup Global Markets
summary: Johanna Chua of Citigroup Global Markets discusses the outlook for monetary policy and energy prices. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Qatar Brings Empty LNG Ships Through Hormuz as Exports Rise
summary: Qatar is rushing to bring home more empty liquefied natural gas tankers as the nation prepares to restart shipments accounting for about a fifth of global supply.

title: Iran Deal 'Strategic Defeat' for US: Defense Analyst
summary: Stimson Center Senior Fellow Kelly Grieco argues the new memorandum of understanding largely restores the prewar status quo rather than delivering decisive gains for the United States, calling it a "strategic defeat" for the US. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chinese Stocks in Hong Kong Near Bear Market After Holiday
summary: A gauge of Chinese stocks in Hong Kong edged toward a bear market as trading resumed after the holiday, weighed by weak consumption data and investors chasing AI shares elsewhere.

title: Gold Advances After Negotiators Flag Headway in US-Iran Talks
summary: Gold rose after negotiators heralded progress in talks between the US and Iran to find a permanent resolution to the war that’s roiled global markets.

title: Aluminum’s War Shock Blunted by Dark Transits and Chinese Supply
summary: The Iran war caused one of the biggest supply shocks to ever hit the aluminum market, but the runaway price surge that many were bracing for has been blunted by the ingenuity of producers from the Middle East to China.

title: Pound Trades Near 2026 Low as UK Political Uncertainty Builds
summary: The pound traded near this year’s low on expectations Keir Starmer will set out a timetable for his departure as UK prime minister in coming days.

title: Vale Board Resists Shareholder Previ’s Bid to Oust Chairman
summary: Board members of Vale SA, the world’s top iron ore producer, voted against a proposal by one of the company’s largest shareholders to remove Daniel André Stieler as chairman, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Treasuries Decline as Trump’s Iran Threats Stoke Inflation Fears
summary: Treasuries fell after President Donald Trump again threatened military action against Iran over Hezbollah’s attacks on Israel, sending oil prices higher and prompting investors to reassess inflation risks.

title: Inghams Shares Tumble as Farms Locked Down in Fight Against H5N1
summary: Shares in Australia-listed chicken producer Inghams Group Ltd. dropped as much as 14% in early Monday trading after the company said it had locked down its Western Australia operations following the detection of the H5N1 avian influenza in the state.
